option_2.rationale: If seizure burden is very high and immediate control is needed, continue phenobarbital (accessible, already in use) while initiating valproate, then reassess for later simplification.

option_3.label: If valproate is not feasible (contraindication/unavailable), use phenobarbital-centered regimen and remove carbamazepine
option_3.rationale: When valproate cannot be used, phenobarbital remains a practical baseline and clobazam is a reasonable adjunct for frequent generalized convulsive seizures without relying on carbamazepine.
